documents.title: University of California, Santa Cruz public AI posture sources

ai_strategy.summary: UC Santa Cruz has a coherent 2026 AI governance posture: UC Responsible AI Principles, an AI Council, a Generative AI Center, Baskin Engineering AI research, and research-computing support.

key_provisions: Use institutionally reviewed AI tools for sensitive university work.
Protect restricted, confidential, student, personnel, and research data when using AI tools.
Verify AI output and keep humans accountable for academic, administrative, and research decisions.
ai_policy.summary: University of California, Santa Cruz's public AI policy posture is comprehensive and emphasizes responsible use, privacy, and human accountability based on reviewed sources.

bodies.name: Generative AI Center
bodies.scope: UC Santa Cruz has a coherent 2026 AI governance posture: UC Responsible AI Principles, an AI Council, a Generative AI Center, Baskin Engineering AI research, and research-computing support.

governance_structure.summary: Governance appears distributed across AI-facing offices, centers, technology units, teaching units, or committees; no standalone chief AI officer was confirmed unless named in leadership.

initiatives.title: Responsible AI guidance and governance
initiatives.description: University of California, Santa Cruz has public AI guidance, governance, or strategy resources that frame responsible adoption, privacy, academic integrity, and human oversight.
initiatives.significance: This is a concrete R1 signal of AI moving from isolated experimentation toward governed, mission-linked adoption.

investment.description: No public dollar amount found in reviewed sources.

initiatives.policy_implications: Requires privacy, academic-integrity, and human-oversight controls as AI use scales.

tools_and_vendors.name: Google NotebookLM
tools_and_vendors.role: Licensed AI research assistant for staff.

initiatives.policy_implications: Access is limited to staff pending Academic Senate and stakeholder consultation for faculty and students.
initiatives.title: Staff Gemini and NotebookLM licensed AI tools
initiatives.description: In February 2026, UCSC enabled campus-licensed Google Gemini Chat and NotebookLM for staff at no cost through the UCSC Google Workspace account, with consultation underway for faculty and student access.
initiatives.significance: This adds a named licensed-platform rollout while preserving the limited audience and governance caveat.

investment.description: No public dollar amount found in cited source.

initiatives.policy_implications: Requires users to ask whether AI use creates inequity, whether students have equal access or opt-out alternatives, and how implementations will be assessed.
initiatives.title: UC AI principles guiding questions for teaching and assessment
initiatives.description: UCSC’s AI Council adopted UC Responsible AI Principles and published guiding questions for teaching, research, and administration, including equal access, opt-outs, student-facing accuracy, bias, privacy, assessment plans, and alternatives to AI support.
initiatives.significance: This supplies explicit assessment and student-impact guardrails even before broad faculty/student licensed-tool rollout.

research_centers.name: Generative AI Center
research_centers.focus: GenAI technology, applications, policy, and education

ai_academic_programs.name: Artificial Intelligence for Researchers pre-college course

computing_infrastructure.summary: UC Santa Cruz ITS lists Hummingbird as a no-cost open-access HPC cluster for faculty and researchers, Elkhorn as an HPC cluster for climate research, and National Research Platform Nautilus as a GPU-focused self-service resource for UCSC researchers. Public service pages confirm AI/HPC availability but do not publish a stable campus GPU count.

regulatory_context.summary: State/system context was checked for CA. Relevant public AI or system governance links are included where found.

narrative_summary: UC Santa Cruz now has clearer public signals for licensed AI tools, governance, and research computing. Staff gained no-cost Google Gemini Chat and NotebookLM access in February 2026 under UCSC Google Workspace terms, while faculty and student access remains under consultation. Research computing pages list Hummingbird, Elkhorn, and Nautilus as HPC/GPU-oriented resources for UCSC researchers, though no stable GPU count is published. UCSC’s AI Council guiding questions add assessment and equity guardrails, including equal access, opt-out options, bias, privacy, student-facing accuracy, and assessment plans. Remaining gaps are exact user counts, contract values, all-campus GPU capacity, and subgroup outcome metrics.

site:its.ucsc.edu secure artificial intelligence tools enabled for staff Gemini NotebookLM
site:its.ucsc.edu Hummingbird open access cluster UC Santa Cruz HPC
site:campusai.ucsc.edu guiding questions UC AI principles assessment student access
gaps_acknowledged: No public chief AI officer was confirmed in reviewed sources.
Licensed Gemini and NotebookLM access is public for staff, but faculty/student access timing, active-user counts, and contract values were not disclosed.
Hummingbird, Elkhorn, and Nautilus access are public, but a current campuswide GPU count and student subgroup outcome metrics were not found.

strategic_archetype.rationale: Primary archetype selected from visible public signals: governance led.
